title: Elatine gyrosperma
intro: Elatine gyrosperma, also known as the gyrosperma water-willow, is a rare and aquatic plant species that thrives in shallow, freshwater environments. Research has shown that Elatine gyrosperma possesses potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Elatine gyrosperma is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Elatine gyrosperma is a species of flowering plant in the family Elatinaceae. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae, a group of organisms commonly known as plants. The plant's classification is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Rosids, order Myrtales, family Elatinaceae, genus Elatine, and species E. gyrosperma. Further, it is a member of the genus Elatine, which contains approximately 30 species of aquatic plants. Elatine gyrosperma is native to Europe and Asia, where it is found in freshwater environments.
constituents: Elatine gyrosperma is a plant species with reported medicinal properties. The active constituents of Elatine gyrosperma have not been extensively studied, but it contains various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. These compounds are believed to contribute to the plant's potential therapeutic effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the active constituents and their pharmacological properties. Elatine gyrosperma may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties due to its bioactive compounds.
parts: Elatine gyrosperma, also known as the circular-leaf water-witch, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine. The medicinal parts of Elatine gyrosperma are primarily its leaves and stems. The plant contains various bioactive compounds that have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. Traditionally, the leaves of Elatine gyrosperma have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal applications of Elatine gyrosperma.
